content: Attack chain and combo animation timing in turn-based RPGs: In an idle RPG with REST polling, the timing model is: server computes full round → state arrives at client → client plays back animations for that round. Attack chains (multi-hit combos, multi-target attacks) become sequences in the animation queue. Pattern: each 'attack event' in the chain is a separate queue item. For a hero that attacks 3 times, the queue has: step1 (attack swing), step2 (impact flash + float text), step3 (attack swing), step4 (impact flash + float text), step5 (attack swing), step6 (impact flash + crit float text). The timing is fixed per step (not variable) since the server already resolved all hits. For simultaneous actions (all heroes attack in one round), steps that are from different actors can be parallel. The CombatDiff function produces 'tracks' per actor that the queue interleaves. This avoids the current pattern where playAttackAnimation fire-and-forgets with setTimeout and all animations overlap chaotically.
